raw_response:
I do agree that option C "Molars and incisors" best describes where periodontosis typically manifests due to its widespread impact across different tooth types rather than being localized to just one area like premolars. While other regions can also be affected, specifying both molars and incisors captures the broad range more accurately since periodontosis tends to affect multiple areas of the mouth similarly.

Therefore, my final answer is still **Option C**. 

This expansion clarifies how each type was considered but ultimately supports Option C as the correct choice because periodontosis does indeed have a broader distribution involving both molars and incisors extensively. Given no additional nuances need addressing beyond what was initially stated, sticking closely to the core support makes sense under our guidelines. --- If any adjustments needed or if expanding again would help refine understanding!
